title: Multi-Source Property Transaction Data
input:
PROPERTY TRANSACTION — MULTIPLE SOURCES

=== MLS LISTING ===

Property Address: 2890 Oak Ridge Lane, Carmel Valley, CA 92130
MLS#: 260015847
Listed: March 10, 2026
List Price: $2,195,000
Status: Active — Under Contract (Escrow)

Property Details:
  Type: Single Family Residence
  Year Built: 2019
  Sq Ft: 3,840 (per listing agent)
  Bedrooms: 4 + office/den
  Bathrooms: 3.5
  Lot Size: 6,534 sq ft
  Garage: 2-car attached
  HOA: $185/month
  Pool: None
  View: Valley/Day
  Condition: Excellent
  Days on Market: 18

Features: Open floor plan, chef's kitchen (Quartz counters, Wolf gas range), hardwood floors, smart home automation, evaporative cooling, drought-tolerant landscaping.

School District: Poway Unified — listed as rated A (GreatSchools: 9/10)

=== BUYER PRE-APPROVAL LETTER ===

Pacific National Bank
Pre-Approved for a Mortgage Loan up to $2,300,000
Borrower: David & Rachel Kim
Loan Program: Conventional 30-year fixed
Rate Lock: 6.125% (expires June 15, 2026)
Down Payment: 20% ($460,000)
Approval Date: March 5, 2026

=== ESCROW OPENING (March 28, 2026) ===

Escrow Company: Fidelity National Title
Escrow #78-2026-44122
Escrow Officer: Lisa Tanaka

Opening Deposit: $10,000 from buyer (held by escrow)
Purchase Price (as agreed): $2,150,000
Contingencies:
  — Inspection: April 11, 2026
  — Appraisal: April 15, 2026
  — Financing: April 20, 2026
  — Title Review: April 5, 2026

Closing Date (estimated): May 15, 2026

=== INSPECTION REPORT (April 12, 2026) ===

Inspector: Certified Home Inspections, Inc.
Inspector: Robert Chang, CA License #HI-4821

Findings:
  — HVAC filter needs replacement (minor, $45)
  — Backyard drip irrigation zone 3 leaking — needs repair ($300-$500 est.)
  — Kitchen garbage disposal humming but not grinding — may need replacement (~$250)
  — No structural, electrical, or plumbing deficiencies noted
  — Foundation: solid, no cracks or settling
  — Roof: good condition, estimated 8-12 years remaining life

Overall rating: Good condition. Minor repairs only.

=== PRICE REDUCTION REQUEST ===

Buyer's agent letter (April 14, 2026):

"We request a $12,000 credit at closing for the following:
  — Inspection repairs (est. $800-1,000)
  — Buyer's appraisal came in at $2,100,000 (gap of $50,000 from purchase price)
  — We're willing to cover the appraisal gap up to $30,000 if seller contributes the rest"

Seller's counter-offer (April 16, 2026):
"Seller agrees to $5,000 in repair credits. Appraisal gap: seller willing to meet halfway on first $10,000 only."

Negotiations ongoing as of April 18.
